title: hieracium sabaudum subsp. sabaudiforme
intro: Hieracium sabaudum subsp. sabaudiforme, also known as the yellow hawkweed or yellow devil's paintbrush, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling, promote wound healing, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Hieracium sabaudum subsp. sabaudiforme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular remedy for colds, flu, and gastrointestinal problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, or used as a poultice to apply directly to affected areas.
